title: lupinus lyallii subsp. subpandens
intro: Lupinus lyallii subsp. subpandens, also known as the Lyall's lupin, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae, native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lupinus lyallii subsp. subpandens include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
